description:
You are a storage administrator who has just received three daily disk-usage reports in JSON
    format.  
    A draft JSON-Schema for the reports already exists on the machine so that you can check every
    incoming document for structural correctness before you build any statistics.

    Directories and files that are already present:

      1. /home/user/storage_audit/schema/disk_report.schema.json
      2. /home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/daily_storage01.json
      3. /home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/daily_storage02.json
      4. /home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/daily_storage03.json

    What you have to do:

      A. Validation stage  
         1. Validate every *.json file that is located inside
            /home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/ **against the schema file mentioned above**.  
         2. Produce a validation log at
            /home/user/storage_audit/logs/validation.log  
            • Create the directory “logs” if it does not yet exist.  
            • The log must contain exactly one line per file with the following fixed format  
              (no extra spaces, tabs or characters):
                   <fileName> : <result>[ : <errorMessage>]  
              where  
                <fileName>    = the base name of the file that was checked  
                <result>       = the literal string OK or ERROR  
                <errorMessage> (only when result = ERROR) = the first error message emitted by
                                   the validator, **no colons** in that message.
              Examples:  
                   daily_storage01.json : OK  
                   broken.json : ERROR : required property "host" missing

      B. Aggregation & reporting stage  
         1. Ignore every file that was logged as ERROR in the previous step.  
         2. For every **valid** mount entry, calculate  
              freeGB        = totalGB – usedGB  
              usagePercent  = (usedGB / totalGB) × 100,   rounded **to two decimal places**.  
         3. Build a single summary file at  
            /home/user/storage_audit/summary/disk_usage_summary.json  
            (create the directory “summary” if necessary) that follows **exactly** the
            structure below – field order, letter case, and indentation matter because
            automated tests will read it verbatim:

    {
      "generatedAt": "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z",
      "totals": {
        "totalGB": <number>,
        "usedGB": <number>,
        "freeGB": <number>,
        "usagePercent": <number with two decimals>
      },
      "mounts": [
        {
          "host": "<host>",
          "mountPoint": "<mount>",
          "totalGB": <number>,
          "usedGB": <number>,
          "freeGB": <number>,
          "usagePercent": <number with two decimals>
        }
        … further objects, one for **every** individual mount, see rules below …
      ]
    }

            • generatedAt       MUST remain the static value
              “2023-01-01T00:00:00Z” so that tests have a deterministic field.  
            • totals.totalGB    = arithmetic sum of all totalGB values of
                                  every mount contained in every valid file.  
            • totals.usedGB     = sum of all usedGB values.  
            • totals.freeGB     = sum of all freeGB values.  
            • totals.usagePercent= (totals.usedGB / totals.totalGB) × 100,
                                  rounded to **two** decimals.  
            • The “mounts” array must be sorted **descending** by the
              mount-level usagePercent (highest utilisation first).  
            • Every numeric literal in the document must have **either zero
              or exactly two** digits after the decimal point.  Examples: 1850, 58.33

      C. Final console output  
         After the summary is ready, print the absolute path of the file you created so
         that a human operator (and our grading bot) can quickly open it.

    Notes & recommendations:

      • You can use any command-line JSON-Schema validator that is available in the
        container (e.g. `ajv-cli`) or you may install one into your home directory
        during runtime.  
      • `jq` is already available and is perfectly suited for the aggregation work.  
      • Do not modify the schema or the raw report files – treat them as read-only.  
      • You do not have root privileges.  All work must stay inside /home/user.  
      • Make sure the newly created directories (logs, summary) have at least
        755 permissions and the files 644 so that the grader process can read them.

    Success criteria that the automated tests will check:

      1. /home/user/storage_audit/logs/validation.log exists and follows the exact
         format rules defined above.  
      2. /home/user/storage_audit/summary/disk_usage_summary.json exists, is valid
         JSON and its content matches the numeric values and ordering that result
         from the data set in the raw_reports directory (tests know those reference
         values).  
      3. The summary file contains the required static generatedAt timestamp.  
      4. The mounts array is sorted correctly.  
      5. All numeric fields obey the two-decimal rule.

    You do not need to write any additional documentation or comments—just the two
    artefacts (validation log and summary JSON) in the exact locations and formats
    specified above.
truth:
<!--
      Ground-truth data used by the automated test suite.
      DO NOT expose any of this in the public task description above.
  -->

  <!-- Pre-seeded schema -->
  <file path="/home/user/storage_audit/schema/disk_report.schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["host", "generatedAt", "mounts"],
  "properties": {
    "host": { "type": "string" },
    "generatedAt": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },
    "mounts": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": ["mountPoint", "totalGB", "usedGB"],
        "properties": {
          "mountPoint": { "type": "string" },
          "totalGB": { "type": "number" },
          "usedGB": { "type": "number" }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
  ]]></file>

  <!-- Pre-seeded raw reports -->
  <file path="/home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/daily_storage01.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "host": "storage01",
  "generatedAt": "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "mounts": [
    { "mountPoint": "/",      "totalGB": 50,  "usedGB": 25 },
    { "mountPoint": "/data",  "totalGB": 200, "usedGB": 150 }
  ]
}
  ]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/daily_storage02.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "host": "storage02",
  "generatedAt": "2023-01-01T00:05:00Z",
  "mounts": [
    { "mountPoint": "/",       "totalGB": 60,  "usedGB": 35 },
    { "mountPoint": "/backup", "totalGB": 500, "usedGB": 400 }
  ]
}
  ]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/storage_audit/raw_reports/daily_storage03.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "host": "storage03",
  "generatedAt": "2023-01-01T00:10:00Z",
  "mounts": [
    { "mountPoint": "/",        "totalGB": 40,  "usedGB": 10 },
    { "mountPoint": "/archive", "totalGB": 1000,"usedGB": 600 }
  ]
}
  ]]></file>

  <!-- Expected artefacts created by the agent -->

  <file path="/home/user/storage_audit/logs/validation.log" must_create="true"><![CDATA[
daily_storage01.json : OK
daily_storage02.json : OK
daily_storage03.json : OK
  ]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/storage_audit/summary/disk_usage_summary.json" must_create="true"><![CDATA[
{
  "generatedAt": "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "totals": {
    "totalGB": 1850,
    "usedGB": 1220,
    "freeGB": 630,
    "usagePercent": 65.95
  },
  "mounts": [
    {
      "host": "storage02",
      "mountPoint": "/backup",
      "totalGB": 500,
      "usedGB": 400,
      "freeGB": 100,
      "usagePercent": 80.00
    },
    {
      "host": "storage01",
      "mountPoint": "/data",
      "totalGB": 200,
      "usedGB": 150,
      "freeGB": 50,
      "usagePercent": 75.00
    },
    {
      "host": "storage03",
      "mountPoint": "/archive",
      "totalGB": 1000,
      "usedGB": 600,
      "freeGB": 400,
      "usagePercent": 60.00
    },
    {
      "host": "storage02",
      "mountPoint": "/",
      "totalGB": 60,
      "usedGB": 35,
      "freeGB": 25,
      "usagePercent": 58.33
    },
    {
      "host": "storage01",
      "mountPoint": "/",
      "totalGB": 50,
      "usedGB": 25,
      "freeGB": 25,
      "usagePercent": 50.00
    },
    {
      "host": "storage03",
      "mountPoint": "/",
      "totalGB": 40,
      "usedGB": 10,
      "freeGB": 30,
      "usagePercent": 25.00
    }
  ]
}
  ]]></file>
